race_name: L'Etape Italy Piemonte by Tour de France

top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic L'Etape Italy Piemonte by Tour de France's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
When someone attacks on alpine cycle-touring with moderate climbing and, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
L'Etape Italy Piemonte by Tour de France's alpine cycle-touring with moderate climbing and forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 31 miles.
31 miles on alpine cycle-touring with moderate climbing and burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what L'Etape Italy Piemonte by Tour de France's alpine cycle-touring with moderate climbing and actually does to you.
84ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
2-minute all-out efforts on alpine cycle-touring with moderate climbing and. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of L'Etape Italy Piemonte by Tour de France. Train it until it feels boring.
Base fitness for L'Etape Italy Piemonte by Tour de France. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At L'Etape Italy Piemonte by Tour de France, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
At L'Etape Italy Piemonte by Tour de France, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.

race_overlay.nutrition: Standard hydration and fueling. One bottle per hour minimum. Gel or bar every 45 minutes at race intensity.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at L'Etape Italy Piemonte by Tour de France. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, VO2max, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 31 miles of alpine cycle-touring with moderate climbing and scenic variety in Entracque, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italy.
